
\color{blue}

**Solution**. A.\

It is necessary to split the random number stream, e.g.,
```python
    key_SI, key_IR = jax.random.split(key)
```
Otherwise, the same random number key is used to determine both the SI and IR transitions, which does not have any reasonable scientific justification.
We would be fitting an unintended model with unknown consequences.
Points (B,C,D) are all reasonable concerns, but not necessarily fatal. 
(B) is usually recommended, but only sometimes critical depending on whether the resulting `p` ever exceeds 1.
(C) is also common, but sometimes the measurements directly correspond to prevalence (I) rather than incidence (new arrivals in I).
(D) is also good advice, escpecially if the population is large so demographic stochasticity is relatively irrelevant, but we don't know if that is the case here.

\color{black}
